class.name: SQLiteColumnSchema
class.brief_description: Represents the schema definition of a database column.
class.description: Describes a single column in a database table, including its name, type, constraints, and default value. This class is used when defining or inspecting table schemas.

method.name: create
method.description:
Creates and returns a new [SQLiteColumnSchema] instance with the specified properties.
The column name is defined by [param name], and its data type is given by [param type] using [enum Variant.Type]. The [param default_value] is used when no explicit value is provided during insertion.
If [param primary_key] is [code]true[/code], the column is marked as the table's primary key. Enabling [param auto_increment] makes the column automatically increment for each inserted row (typically used with integer primary keys).
If [param not_null] is [code]true[/code], the column cannot contain [code]NULL[/code] values. If [param unique] is [code]true[/code], all values in the column must be distinct.

members.member: If [code]true[/code], the column value is automatically incremented for each new row.
The default value assigned to the column when no value is provided.
If [code]true[/code], the column cannot contain [code]NULL[/code] values.
If [code]true[/code], the column is part of the table's primary key.
The data type of the column, defined using [code]Variant.Type[/code].
If [code]true[/code], all values in the column must be unique.
